p3.title: Partial negative-path test coverage for fail-closed normalization
p3.line_or_area: Hermes Evidence Packet request normalization tests (createHermesEvidencePacketRequestInput + 5 tests)
p3.issue: The denied-path test exercises only a subset of guard branches (entrypoint, source micro-phase, contract status, closeout status, closeout ref, command_receipt_fields drift, one forbidden claim, one unknown claim). Untested branches include: missing required input field, non-object/array request, inputClaims supplied as an array, whitespace-padded string rejection (the `!== trim()` check), blocked_claim_refs non-array/blank entries, and drift in the other array-equality fields (hermes_evidence_packet_context_ids, required_evidence_refs, schema_expectation_fields, evidence_summary_fields, blocked_claim_fields, gate_outcome_fields, acceptance_gate_ids).
p3.recommendation: Add focused assert.throws cases for the remaining guard branches so the fail-closed contract is locked against future regressions; behavior is currently correct but under-pinned by tests.

p3.title: Some declarative scope_policy flags are not cross-validated by the contract validator
p3.issue: contract scope_policy declares does_not_mutate_entity_registry, does_not_replace_human_approval, does_not_replace_H00_or_C00, and a nested no_real_data, but the validator's scope_flag loop does not assert these (the human-approval guarantee is covered indirectly only via the policy module's doesNotReplaceHumanApproval check). These contract assertions could silently drift to false without failing validation.
p3.recommendation: Add the missing scope_policy flags to the validator's scope_flag assertion list for symmetric, fail-closed coverage of the declared boundary.

p3.title: Closeout evidence artifacts for CP00-050 are outside the provided diff
p3.line_or_area: construction inspection record, Hermes evidence, adjudication, command evidence
p3.issue: The validator requires a construction-inspection record with control_plane_hermes_evidence_packet_request_normalization_verified === true (validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs subphase_closeouts loop), and pack closeout requires Hermes evidence, adjudication, and command evidence under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-050. None of these artifacts are in the reviewed diff, so they cannot be verified here.
p3.recommendation: Before commit/closeout, confirm the cp00-050 evidence files exist with the required verified flag and that `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` passes; this is the only remaining gate the code diff cannot self-evidence.

boundary_checks.evidence: Normalized output sets runtime_route_created=false; policy/normalizer carry doesNotCreateRuntimeRoute=true; no route registration code added in service.js.

Only request normalization is performed (normalizeControlPlaneHermesEvidencePacketRequest builds a frozen metadata object); policy doesNotExecuteServiceLogic=true and normalized service_logic_execution_permitted=false.

No Hermes calls; normalized hermes_runtime_execution_permitted/hermes_validation_execution_permitted/hermes_evidence_creation_permitted all false; forbidden claims include run_hermes_validation/execute_hermes_runtime/create_hermes_evidence and fail-closed mapping hermes_runtime_or_evidence_creation_claim.

Function performs in-memory transform only; database_write_permitted/storage_write_permitted/product_state_persistence_permitted=false; writes_product_state=false; only file reads occur in tests/validator.

All values synthetic (tenant.synthetic.lawfirm, user.synthetic.control-plane); fixture_integrity no_real_client_data/no_real_matter_data/no_credentials_or_secrets=true; forbidden claims include use_real_client_data/use_real_matter_data/use_credentials_or_secrets.

replace_human_approval is a forbidden input claim and policy.doesNotReplaceHumanApproval=true (asserted by assertControlPlaneHermesEvidencePacketRequestNormalizationPolicy); normalization records no approvals.

boundary_checks.check: Downstream tenant/matter/permission/audit prechecks deferred, not bypassed
boundary_checks.evidence: Normalized output marks *_precheck_required=true with deferred_to S03/S04/S05/S06; bypass_* are forbidden claims; pass-through of tenant_id/actor_tenant_id without matching is intentional per staged design.

definition.scope_policy.does_not_complete_RP00_P02_M08=true; closeout_state_policy.source_micro_phase_completed=false; fixture completes_source_micro_phase=false; weighted boundary terminal=false; no M08 micro-phase closeout added.
next_subphase=RP00.P02.M08.S03 across contract definition, policy, normalizer, fixture, normalized output, and validator weighted/next-boundary checks.

boundary_checks.check: Exactly one unit, production_ready, Risk class A
boundary_checks.evidence: Single surface ControlPlaneHermesEvidencePacketRequestNormalization marked production_ready in current_subphase, subphase_closeouts, definition, policy, normalizer; new production_ready_requires flag added and gated by validator.

boundary_checks.check: Deterministic, no real I/O side effects
boundary_checks.evidence: No Date.now/random; output is a fixed frozen snapshot; validator live-normalizes the sample and byte-compares JSON to fixture.normalized_request, enforcing determinism.

parsed_review.recommendation: Approve as the single Claude review for CP00-050. The diff is internally consistent and tightly cross-validated (contract definition, policy, normalizer, validators, fixture, and validation script are kept in lockstep with explicit length/order/identity checks), fail-closed on drift and unsafe/unknown claims, and respects every required boundary: no runtime route, no service-logic/Hermes execution, no evidence creation, no writes, synthetic-only data, preserved human-approval boundary, M08 kept open, and clean handoff to S03. No P0/P1/P2 found, so the unit may be marked production_ready. Address the three P3 items as non-blocking follow-ups, and (out of diff scope) confirm the cp00-050 evidence artifacts exist and `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` passes before commit.
